definition: EM equity valuations are claimed to embed a 15-40% discount attributable to FX regime risk. The range is too wide to be informative: 15% implies modest FX risk pricing while 40% implies existential regime threat. Governance, liquidity, and growth premia independently justify large discounts, creating an unresolved identification problem.
evidence: Range 15-40% too wide to be informative
pair_1: range spans modest risk pricing to existential threat
Governance, liquidity, and growth premia independently justify large discounts
Attribution to FX regime risk specifically is an unresolved identification problem
open_questions: Can FX regime risk premium be isolated from governance and liquidity premia?
Does the discount vary systematically with de jure/de facto regime mismatch?
What natural experiment or methodology could resolve the identification problem?
